title: Potato Egg Hash

subs.name: sweet potato, diced
subs.notes: cooks slightly faster; sweeter result that balances the chilli well

subs.name: crumbled paneer pressed into the wells
subs.notes: vegetarian — add after potatoes are done and heat through

subs.name: spinach or kale, wilted in
subs.notes: stir in at the end — takes only 1 minute

steps.text: Heat oil in a wide frying pan or tawa on medium-high. Add cumin seeds and let them sputter 10 seconds. Add onion, garlic, and green chilli and sauté 2 minutes.

Add potato cubes, turmeric, chilli powder, and salt. Spread in a single layer and cook without stirring for 4 minutes until the bottom develops a golden crust. Then toss and press flat again. Repeat twice more until the potatoes are golden all over and cooked through when pierced.
Add capsicum and toss for 1 minute until it just softens. Taste the potato and adjust salt.

Make three wells in the potato mixture. Crack one egg into each well. Season the eggs lightly with salt and pepper. Cover the pan with a lid and cook on medium-low until the whites are fully set but the yolks are still soft, about 3–4 minutes.
Remove from heat while the yolks are still glossy — they will continue cooking for a minute off the flame. Scatter coriander leaves over and serve directly from the pan with bread or roti.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
critical_checks: Use pasteurised eggs for preparations served raw or lightly cooked, especially for higher-risk people.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: egg → crumbled paneer pressed into the wells (vegetarian — add after potatoes are done and heat through); capsicum → spinach or kale, wilted in (stir in at the end — takes only 1 minute); potato → sweet potato, diced (cooks slightly faster; sweeter result that balances the chilli well)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: A kadai or tawa works perfectly in place of a cast-iron skillet. Use groundnut oil for a higher smoke point and better crust. If you want runny yolks but lack a lid, cover the pan with a large plate or thali for the final egg-cooking step.
